skill.name: Frontend Security Coder
skill.description: A specialized skill that helps developers write secure frontend code by generating vulnerability assessments, security patches, and best practices for web applications.
skill.summary: Generate secure frontend code and identify vulnerabilities in web applications
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill that generates frontend security content. No executable code was detected. The skill provides security guidance through natural language prompts without any system-level access, network calls, or file operations. Risk score is 0/100.

content.value_statement: Frontend applications are vulnerable to XSS, CSRF, and other attacks. This skill generates secure code patterns and identifies vulnerabilities in your web applications to protect against common attack vectors.
actual_capabilities: Generate XSS-safe code patterns for JavaScript and framework code
Identify potential security vulnerabilities in frontend code
Create CSRF token implementations and validation logic
Produce Content Security Policy headers and configurations
Generate input sanitization functions for user inputs
Provide secure authentication flow guidance for frontend apps
limitations: Does not scan or analyze existing codebases - only generates content from prompts
Cannot detect backend vulnerabilities or server-side issues
Does not perform live security testing or penetration testing
Security recommendations are based on patterns, not runtime analysis

use_cases.title: Secure Component Development
use_cases.description: Generate security-aware React, Vue, or Angular components with built-in XSS protection and input validation

use_cases.title: Security Code Review
use_cases.description: Get detailed security analysis of frontend code snippets with specific vulnerability identification and remediation guidance
use_cases.target_user: Security engineers reviewing web application code

use_cases.title: Security Best Practices Learning
use_cases.description: Learn secure coding patterns and practices for frontend development through generated examples and explanations
use_cases.target_user: Junior developers learning web security fundamentals

prompt_templates.title: Basic XSS Protection
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a XSS-safe input component in [FRAMEWORK] that sanitizes user input and prevents script injection attacks. Include proper escaping functions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a secure input field that protects against cross-site scripting

prompt_templates.title: CSRF Token Handler
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a CSRF token management module for a frontend application. Include token retrieval, validation on requests, and automatic token refresh logic.

prompt_templates.title: Secure Authentication Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a secure authentication flow pattern for a single-page application. Include token storage best practices, refresh token handling, and session timeout logic.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building secure login and session management

prompt_templates.title: CSP Configuration Guide
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a comprehensive Content Security Policy configuration for a modern web application. Include directives for scripts, styles, images, and API calls.
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up Content Security Policy headers

output_examples.input: Generate a XSS-safe input component in React that sanitizes user input
output_examples.output: A React component with proper input sanitization using DOMPurify, output encoding, and security-focused prop validation.

output_examples.input: Write CSRF protection code for a JavaScript application
output_examples.output: JavaScript module with fetch wrapper that automatically attaches CSRF tokens, validates responses, and handles token refresh.

output_examples.input: Create a secure authentication pattern for a SPA
output_examples.output: Complete authentication module using httpOnly cookies, refresh tokens with secure storage, and automatic session management.

best_practices: Always validate and sanitize user input on both client and server sides, even if client-side validation is present
Use modern security libraries like DOMPurify for HTML sanitization rather than writing custom escaping functions
Implement Content Security Policy headers to provide an additional layer of defense against XSS attacks
anti_patterns: Using innerHTML with user-provided data without sanitization - this creates direct XSS vulnerabilities
Storing sensitive tokens in localStorage instead of httpOnly cookies - tokens are accessible to JavaScript
Disabling CSRF protection for API endpoints because it adds complexity - this exposes the application to cross-site request forgery

faq.question: Does this skill scan my existing codebase for vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: No, this skill generates security-focused code and patterns from prompts. It does not analyze external codebases.

faq.question: Can this skill replace a security audit?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides coding guidance and patterns. It cannot replace professional security audits or penetration testing.

faq.question: What frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill can generate code for React, Vue, Angular, vanilla JavaScript, and other popular frontend frameworks.

faq.question: Does the generated code work with both frontend and backend?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on frontend security patterns. Backend security requires server-side implementations not covered by this skill.

faq.question: How do I use this with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Activate the skill and provide specific prompts about the security pattern you need. The skill will generate appropriate code and explanations.

faq.question: Are the security recommendations up to date?
faq.answer: The skill generates recommendations based on established security practices. Always verify against current security standards and OWASP guidelines.
